TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy, title = {Sakizaya Wikipedia}, author = {{Wikipedia contributors}}, year = {2026}, publisher = {Wikimedia Foundation}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/}, urldate = {2026-06-09} }

S.FORM: bantek ť≥•ŤłŹťô∑ťėĪ bantek ku sapibihkac tu mubahelay a ayam. siwkay nu bantek u bantek sa, sapisanga' nu auk , ciid nu kiliang atu lait. maitekel ku ayam atu adidiay a aadupen. anu mazipa' kya telis nu auk, mabetil tu ku auk, masiket tu lait, maala tu ku ayam atu adidi'ay a aadupan. tinaku a kamu pabantek śĒĺťô∑ťėĪ pabantek ci Bunuk i buyubuyuan tu elielip. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika Animal trapping, or simply trapping, is the use of a device to remotely catch an animal. Animals may be trapped for a variety of purposes, including food, the fur trade, hunting, pest control, and wildlife management.
S.PHON: bant…ôk **** bant…ôk ku sapibińßka ¶ tu mubańß…ôr~…ĺaj a ajam. siwkaj nu bant…ôk u bant…ôk sa, sapisaŇča Ē~ ° nu auk , ¶iid nu kir~…ĺiaŇč atu r~…ĺait. mait…ôk…ôr~…ĺ ku ajam atu adidiaj a aadup…ôn. anu mazipa Ē~ ° kja t…ôr~…ĺis nu auk, mab…ôtir~…ĺ tu ku auk, masik…ôt tu r~…ĺait, maar~…ĺa tu ku ajam atu adidi Ē~ °aj a aadupan. tinaku a kamu pabant…ôk *** pabant…ôk ¶i bunuk i bujubujuan tu …ôr~…ĺi…ôr~…ĺip. u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika animar~…ĺ trappiŇč, or simpr~…ĺj trappiŇč, is tńß…ô us…ô of a d…ô*i ¶…ô to r…ômot…ôr~…ĺj ¶at ¶ńß an animar~…ĺ. animar~…ĺs maj b…ô trapp…ôd for a *ari…ôtj of purpos…ôs, in ¶r~…ĺudiŇč food, tńß…ô fur trad…ô, ńßuntiŇč, p…ôst ¶ontror~…ĺ, and wir~…ĺdr~…ĺif…ô manag…ôm…ônt.
S.FORM: bantek ť≥•ŤłŹťô∑ťėĪ bantek ku sapibihkac tu mubahelay a ayam. siwkay nu bantek u bantek sa, sapisanga' nu auk , ciid nu kiliang atu lait. maitekel ku ayam atu adidiay a aadupen. anu mazipa' kya telis nu auk, mabetil tu ku auk, masiket tu lait, maala tu ku ayam atu adidi'ay a aadupan. tinaku a kamu pabantek śĒĺťô∑ťėĪ pabantek ci Bunuk i buyubuyuan tu elielip. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika Animal trapping, or simply trapping, is the use of a device to remotely catch an animal. Animals may be trapped for a variety of purposes, including food, the fur trade, hunting, pest control, and wildlife management.
